Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar May-15-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT JAMES LODUCA, CHARLES SMITH NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS is GRANTED. The Court finds that the attorneys' fees provision as it related to the cross-complaint is not a material settlement term. (Lindsay v. Lewandowski (2006) 139 Cal.App.4th 1618, 1622; see also, Westlands Water District v. All Persons Interested (2023) 95 Cal.App.5th 98, 127 [defining "material term"].) The Court further finds that Plaintiff has not met the elements to obtain rescission. (Donovan v. RRL Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 261, 263-64.) The Court does not find the resulting imbalance in the agreed exchange so severe that it would be unfair to require the defendant to perform, nor does it find that it is more advantageous to the other party. (Id. at 282.) The parties understood that it was Defendant's intent to proceed on their cross-claim. Defendant could win or they could lose. They could win on some of their claims or on just one of their claims, in which case the court could decide that there is no prevailing party. It has also not been established, through admissible evidence, that the insurance carrier will not pay the attorney's fees. Further, the Court finds that Plaintiff was aware, at the time the contract was made, that he only had limited knowledge with respect to the facts to which the mistake relates but treated his limited knowledge as sufficient. (Id. at 283; see Keane declaration, para's. 4-5.) Finally, the results here are not overly harsh or one sided as to be unconscionable. Plaintiff received $50,000 towards its $85,000 claim and the parties reserved their respective rights to pursue and defend the separate cross-complaint. Moreover, both parties retain the right to seek fees on the cross-complaint if it is the prevailing party. Defendant is awarded reasonable attorneys' fees in the amount of $4,750 and $80 in costs incurred to enforce the settlement agreement.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
